title: BunÂ­desnetÂ­zaÂ­genÂ­tur Sets Ramp-Up TarÂ­iff for HyÂ­droÂ­gen Core NetÂ­work
content: The ramp-up tariff is a uniform tariff applicable throughout Germany that customers of the core network have to pay for the injection and offtake of hydrogen. The tariff must allow the network costs incurred up to 2055 to be recouped. At the same time it must be marketable. The tariff set at â‚¬25/kWh/h/a is the result of a qualified analysis of various scenarios for the development of the hydrogen market. The Bundesnetzagentur, with expert support, produced forecasts about network infrastructure build, the associated costs and the current likely development of the hydrogen market.
author: Analysts Account
